children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established, as an independent office in the executive branch, the Office of the Federal Coordinator for Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Projects.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Office shall be headed by a Federal Coordinator for Alaska Natural Gas Transportation Projects, who shall be appointed by the President, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate, to serve a term to last until 1 year following the completion of the project referred to in .section 720a of this title

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 2 Compensation
children.text: The Federal Coordinator shall be compensated at the rate prescribed for level III of the Executive Schedule ().5 U.S.C. 5314
coordinating the expeditious discharge of all activities by Federal agencies with respect to an Alaska natural gas transportation project; and
ensuring the compliance of Federal agencies with the provisions of this chapter.

children.heading: Reviews and actions of other Federal agencies
children.label: d Reviews and actions of other Federal agencies

children.heading: Expedited reviews and actions
children.label: 1 Expedited reviews and actions
children.text: All reviews conducted and actions taken by any Federal agency relating to an Alaska natural gas transportation project authorized under this section shall be expedited, in a manner consistent with completion of the necessary reviews and approvals by the deadlines under this chapter.

children.heading: Prohibition of certain terms and conditions
children.label: 2 Prohibition of certain terms and conditions
children.text: No Federal agency may include in any certificate, right-of-way, permit, lease, or other authorization issued to an Alaska natural gas transportation project any term or condition that may be permitted, but is not required, by any applicable law if the Federal Coordinator determines that the term or condition would prevent or impair in any significant respect the expeditious construction and operation, or an expansion, of the Alaska natural gas transportation project.

children.heading: Prohibition of certain actions
children.label: 3 Prohibition of certain actions
children.text: Unless required by law, no Federal agency shall add to, amend, or abrogate any certificate, right-of-way, permit, lease, or other authorization issued to an Alaska natural gas transportation project if the Federal Coordinator determines that the action would prevent or impair in any significant respect the expeditious construction and operation, or an expansion, of the Alaska natural gas transportation project.

children.label: i
children.text: the implementation or enforcement of regulations issued by the Commission under ; orsection 720a of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an order by the Commission to expand the project under ; orsection 720c of this title

children.label: B
children.text: impose any terms, conditions, or requirements in addition to those imposed by the Commission or any agency with respect to construction and operation, or an expansion of, the project.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Federal Coordinator and the State shall enter into a joint surveillance and monitoring agreement similar to the agreement in effect during construction of the Trans-Alaska Pipeline, to be approved by the President and the Governor of the State, for the purpose of monitoring the construction of the Alaska natural gas transportation project.

children.label: A
children.text: the Federal Government shall have primary surveillance and monitoring responsibility in areas where the Alaska natural gas transportation project crosses Federal land or private land; and

children.label: B
children.text: the State government shall have primary surveillance and monitoring responsibility in areas where the Alaska natural gas transportation project crosses State land.

children.heading: Transfer of Federal Inspector functions and authority
children.label: f Transfer of Federal Inspector functions and authority
children.text: On appointment of the Federal Coordinator by the President, all of the functions and authority of the Office of Federal Inspector of Construction for the Alaska Natural Gas Transportation System vested in the Secretary under section 3012(b) of the Energy Policy Act of 1992 ( note; ), including all functions and authority described and enumerated in the Reorganization Plan No. 1 of 1979 (44 Fed. Reg. 33663), Executive Order No. 12142 of (44 Fed. Reg. 36927), and section 5 of the President’s decision, shall be transferred to the Federal Coordinator.15 U.S.C. 719ePublic Law 102–486June 21, 1979

children.heading: Temporary authority
children.label: g Temporary authority
children.text: The functions, authorities, duties, and responsibilities of the Federal Coordinator shall be vested in the Secretary until the earlier of the appointment of the Federal Coordinator by the President, or 18 months after .October 13, 2004

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Federal Coordinator may appoint and terminate such personnel as the Federal Coordinator determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Authority of Federal Coordinator
children.label: B Authority of Federal Coordinator
children.text: Personnel appointed by the Federal Coordinator under subparagraph (A) shall be appointed without regard to the provisions of title 5 governing appointments in the competitive service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), personnel appointed by the Federal Coordinator under paragraph (1)(A) shall be paid without regard to the provisions of chapter 51 and subchapter III of chapter 53 of title 5 (relating to classification and General Schedule pay rates).

children.heading: Maximum level of compensation
children.label: B Maximum level of compensation
children.text: The rate of pay for personnel appointed by the Federal Coordinator under paragraph (1)(A) shall not exceed the maximum level of rate payable for level III of the Executive Schedule ().5 U.S.C. 5314

children.heading: Allowances
children.label: C Allowances
children.text: shall apply to personnel appointed by the Federal Coordinator under paragraph (1)(A).Section 5941 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Federal Coordinator may procure temporary and intermittent services in accordance with .section 3109(b) of title 5

children.heading: Maximum level of compensation
children.label: B Maximum level of compensation
children.text: The level of compensation of an individual employed on a temporary or intermittent basis under subparagraph (A) shall not exceed the maximum level of rate payable for level III of the Executive Schedule ().5 U.S.C. 5314

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: With respect to the duties of the Federal Coordinator, as described in this chapter, the Federal Coordinator shall have similar authority to establish, change, and abolish reasonable filing and service fees, charges, and commissions, require deposits of payments, and provide refunds as provided to the Secretary of the Interior in .section 1734 of title 43

children.heading: Authority of Secretary of the Interior
children.label: B Authority of Secretary of the Interior
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not affect the authority of the Secretary of the Interior to establish, change, and abolish reasonable filing and service fees, charges, and commissions, require deposits of payments, and provide refunds under .section 1734 of title 43

children.heading: Use of funds
children.label: C Use of funds
children.text: The Federal Coordinator is authorized to use, without further appropriation, amounts collected under subparagraph (A) to carry out this section.
